app.name: GitHub
app.description: Connect GitHub REST and GraphQL APIs for repositories, issues, pull requests, and file contents.

files.content:
id: github
name: GitHub
version: 1.0.0
description: Access GitHub repositories, issues, pull requests, and GraphQL data through configured endpoints.
auth:
  required: true
  methods:
    - id: github-oauth
      type: oauth2
      provider: github
      label: GitHub OAuth
      default: true
    - id: github-pat
      type: bearer
      label: Personal access token
icon:
  svg: assets/icon.svg
  color:
    light: "#111111"
    dark: "#ffffff"
  background:
    light: "#ffffff"
    dark: "#111111"
endpoints:
  github_rest:
    kind: rest
    url: https://api.github.com
    description: GitHub REST API.
  github_graphql:
    kind: graphql
    url: https://api.github.com/graphql
    description: GitHub GraphQL API.
skills:
  - skills/repositories/SKILL.md
  - skills/issues/SKILL.md

---
name: github-repositories
description: Use when the user asks to inspect GitHub repositories, branches, commits, contents, pull requests, or repository metadata through a connected GitHub app.
---

# GitHub Repositories

Use this skill when a GitHub connection is available and the user asks about repository data.

## Endpoints

- Use `github_rest` with `builtin_rest_request` for REST paths under `https://api.github.com`.
- Use `github_graphql` with `builtin_graphql_request` for GraphQL queries.

## REST examples

- Repository metadata: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}`
- Branches: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches`
- Pull requests: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls`
- File contents: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path}`

## Guidance

- Ask for the repository owner/name if it is not clear from context.
- Prefer GraphQL when the request needs several related resources in one response.
- Do not mutate GitHub state unless the user explicitly asks for a write operation.

---
name: github-issues
description: Use when the user asks to find, summarize, triage, or inspect GitHub issues and issue comments through a connected GitHub app.
---

# GitHub Issues

Use this skill when a GitHub connection is available and the user asks about GitHub issues.

## Endpoints

- Use `github_rest` with `builtin_rest_request` for REST paths under `https://api.github.com`.
- Use `github_graphql` with `builtin_graphql_request` when labels, assignees, comments, and timeline data are needed together.

## REST examples

- List repository issues: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues`
- Read one issue: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue_number}`
- Issue comments: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue_number}/comments`

## Guidance

- Ask for the repository owner/name when it is missing.
- Keep triage summaries short and separate facts from recommendations.
- Do not create, edit, close, label, or comment on issues unless the user explicitly asks.
